title: Define an OIDC Client
description:
## Register a Confidential Web App

A server-side web application needs to delegate login to Keycloak. That means a **confidential** OpenID Connect client using the Authorization Code flow.

**Your task:** write a client definition at **`/root/client.json`** with:
1. **`clientId`** = `falcon-app`
2. **`protocol`** = `openid-connect`
3. **`publicClient`** = `false` (confidential — it can keep a secret)
4. **`standardFlowEnabled`** = `true` (Authorization Code flow)
5. a non-empty **`redirectUris`** array

> 💡 A confidential client has `publicClient: false`; a SPA/mobile app would be `true` and use PKCE instead.

hints.title: Confidential OIDC client JSON
hints.body: Set the protocol, mark it confidential, enable the standard (auth code) flow, and list valid redirect URIs.

validation.description: Checks for a confidential openid-connect client with redirect URIs.

commands.description: Confidential OIDC client 'falcon-app' with redirect URIs
commands.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/root/client.json')); print('ok' if d.get('clientId')=='falcon-app' and d.get('protocol')=='openid-connect' and d.get('publicClient') is False and isinstance(d.get('redirectUris'),list) and len(d['redirectUris'])>0 else 'no')"
